notes.systems-architect: Domain Expertise describes HOW the architect thinks ('dependency graphs, not box diagrams', 'bets on which things change together'). 8 patterns are actionable decision frameworks. Anti-patterns (5 rows) are distinct architecture anti-patterns with clear alternatives. Quality Criteria are testable (no circular deps, interface boundaries at integrations).
notes.security-engineer: Domain Expertise describes adversarial thinking approach ('every input is hostile', 'reasons in layers'). 6 patterns cover the security reasoning spectrum. Anti-patterns (5 rows) are practical and distinct. Quality Criteria specific (parameterized queries, security headers, dependency audit). Context Requirements relevant (auth mechanism, trust boundary map).
